title: anthemis carpatica subsp. petraea
intro: Anthemis carpatica subsp. petraea, also known as the mountain chamomile or alpine chamomile, is a subspecies of Anthemis carpatica, a flowering plant in the daisy family. This perennial plant is native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and calming effects. The leaves and flowers of Anthemis carpatica subsp. petraea have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Herbal preparations of Anthemis carpatica subsp. petraea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
